-module(-solve).
-export([solve/1]).

-end

solve(Nums1) ->
    % 1. Sort nums1
    SortedNums = lists:sort(Nums1),
    
    % Initial state: {H_odd, H_even, C_even, C_odd}
    % H_odd: True if an odd number has been seen before
    % H_even: True if an even number has been seen before
    % C_even: Can ALL elements be made even so far?
    % C_odd: Can ALL elements be made odd so far?
    InitialState = {false, false, true, true},
    
    % 2. Fold over the sorted list
    FinalState = lists:foldl(process_element, InitialState, SortedNums),
    
    % 3. Return the result
    {_, _, C_even, C_odd} = FinalState,
    C_even or C_odd.

%%{ocaml_header}
process_element(State, X) ->
    {H_odd, H_even, C_even_old, C_odd_old} = State,
    
    X_parity = X mod 2,
    
    % --- Calculate P_even (Can X be made even?) ---
    P_even = case X_parity of
        0 -> true; % X is even
        1 -> H_odd % X is odd, requires an odd predecessor S_j (S_i - S_j is even)
        _ -> false
    end,
    
    % --- Calculate P_odd (Can X be made odd?) ---
    P_odd = case X_parity of
        1 -> true; % X is odd
        0 -> H_odd % X is even, requires an odd predecessor S_j (S_i - S_j is odd)
        _ -> false
    end,
    
    % Update global possibilities
    C_even_new = C_even_old and P_even,
    C_odd_new = C_odd_old and P_odd,
    
    % Update prefix history for next iteration
    H_odd_new = H_odd or (X_parity == 1),
    H_even_new = H_even or (X_parity == 0),
    
    {H_odd_new, H_even_new, C_even_new, C_odd_new}.
%%{ocaml_header}

    H_odd_new = H_odd or (X_parity == 1),
    H_even_new = H_even or (X_parity == 0),

    % Partition Nums1 into Evens and Odds.
    % lists:partition(Predicate, List) returns {Elements satisfying Predicate, Elements NOT satisfying Predicate}.

    {Evens, Odds} = lists:partition(fun(X) -> X rem 2 == 0 end, Nums1),

            % Success for E, continue check for the rest of the evens.
            check_p1(RestEvens, Odds);
        false ->
            % Failed for E. P=1 is impossible.
            false
    end.

solve(Nums1) ->
    % Partition Nums1 into Evens and Odds.
    % lists:partition(Predicate, List) returns {Elements satisfying Predicate, Elements NOT satisfying Predicate}.
    % Predicate: is_even(X) = (X rem 2 == 0).
    {Evens, Odds} = lists:partition(fun(X) -> X rem 2 == 0 end, Nums1),

    case {Odds, Evens} of
        % Case 1: Odds list is empty. All numbers are even. P=0 works.
        {[], _} ->
            true;

        % Case 2: Evens list is empty. All numbers are odd. P=1 works.
        {%Odds, []} ->
            true;

        % Case 3: Both lists are non-empty. 
        % P=0 (All Even) is impossible because of the smallest odd number.
        % We must check P=1 (All Odd).
        {Odds, Evens} ->
            % Check if every even number E has an odd predecessor O' < E.
            check_p1(Evens, Odds)
    end.

% Helper function to check P=1 feasibility.
% Checks if for every E in Evens, there exists an O' in Odds such that O' < E.
check_p1([], _Odds) ->
    true;
check_p1([E | RestEvens], Odds) ->
    % Check if any odd number O_prime is strictly less than the current even number E.
    case lists:any(fun(O_prime) -> O_prime < E end, Odds) of
        true ->
            % Success for E, continue check for the rest of the evens.
            check_p1(RestEvens, Odds);
        false ->
            % Failed for E. P=1 is impossible.
            false
    end.
